access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q34.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 2, bài viết nêu: "Traditionally, within any population, there has always been a much larger proportion of people of working age than of elderly people who have stopped work. However, in every developed economy this ratio is becoming much narrower. In the UK, there were 3.7 workers for every elderly person in 1997.". Cụm proportion / ratio khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q34.evidence: Traditionally, within any population, there has always been a much larger proportion of people of working age than of elderly people who have stopped work. However, in every developed economy this ratio is becoming much narrower. In the UK, there were 3.7 workers for every elderly person in 1997._

q35.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 3, bài viết nêu: "The problem with this situation is that workers will have to support a much larger number of people through their taxes. Taxpayers are expected to foot the bill for publicly funded healthcare for all sectors of a population as well as to generate money for the government to maintain and improve infrastructure, and provide national security and education systems. In addition to these demands, the money to pay pensions for an ever-increasing pool of elderly people also comes out of taxes paid by people in employment.". Cụm pensions khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q35.evidence: The problem with this situation is that workers will have to support a much larger number of people through their taxes. Taxpayers are expected to foot the bill for publicly funded healthcare for all sectors of a population as well as to generate money for the government to maintain and improve infrastructure, and provide national security and education systems. In addition to these demands, the money to pay pensions for an ever-increasing pool of elderly people also comes out of taxes paid by people in employment._

q36.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 4, bài viết nêu: "The global population is still increasing due to high birth rates in Africa and some Asian countries. However, in Europe it is only Ireland that is near this target with a birth rate of 2.07, whereas the average across Europe is 1.6. This problem is even more serious in developed Asian countries like South Korea, where a focus on paying for expensive education makes many people believe that they cannot afford more than one child and the birth rate is just under 1.2 per woman.". Cụm birth rate khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q36.evidence: The global population is still increasing due to high birth rates in Africa and some Asian countries. However, in Europe it is only Ireland that is near this target with a birth rate of 2.07, whereas the average across Europe is 1.6. This problem is even more serious in developed Asian countries like South Korea, where a focus on paying for expensive education makes many people believe that they cannot afford more than one child and the birth rate is just under 1.2 per woman._

q37.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 5, bài viết nêu: "However, while people are living longer, they are not necessarily healthier. Elderly people are still much more likely to suffer from illnesses and diseases like diabetes, Alzheimer’s and cancer. Medical advances have meant that these diseases are often not terminal but may require lengthy and expensive medical treatment.". Cụm (medical)treatment khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q37.evidence: However, while people are living longer, they are not necessarily healthier. Elderly people are still much more likely to suffer from illnesses and diseases like diabetes, Alzheimer’s and cancer. Medical advances have meant that these diseases are often not terminal but may require lengthy and expensive medical treatment._

q38.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 6, bài viết nêu: "In many countries, the government is trying to support families and create incentives for people to have children. For example, in Finland, new mothers are given a gift box full of essential items like clothing and nappies. In France, the government provides tax relief and subsidised childcare, initiatives that have helped it to have one of the highest birth rates in Europe.". Cụm incentives khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q38.evidence: In many countries, the government is trying to support families and create incentives for people to have children. For example, in Finland, new mothers are given a gift box full of essential items like clothing and nappies. In France, the government provides tax relief and subsidised childcare, initiatives that have helped it to have one of the highest birth rates in Europe._

q39.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 7, bài viết nêu: "In most developed countries the retirement age is 65 and this has not changed for decades, despite the increase in life expectancy. In some countries, like New Zealand and Japan, a large proportion of those aged between 65-69 are still working. The problem with this is that not all countries have as healthy a population as these countries, which means that people are unable to continue working this long.". Cụm retirement khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q39.evidence: In most developed countries the retirement age is 65 and this has not changed for decades, despite the increase in life expectancy. In some countries, like New Zealand and Japan, a large proportion of those aged between 65-69 are still working. The problem with this is that not all countries have as healthy a population as these countries, which means that people are unable to continue working this long._

q40.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 8, bài viết nêu: "Yet Vitor Constâncio suggests that more immigration is key to increasing economic growth and the population. However, others are less sure, with demography expert Axel Börsch-Supan suggesting that the numbers required to solve the problem would be too massive for a country to deal with. It is certainly true that for migration to be a possible solution individual governments would have to do a great deal more to integrate immigrants into society and help them to be present in all parts of the work force rather than just as unskilled workers.". Cụm economic growth khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q40.evidence: Yet Vitor Constâncio suggests that more immigration is key to increasing economic growth and the population. However, others are less sure, with demography expert Axel Börsch-Supan suggesting that the numbers required to solve the problem would be too massive for a country to deal with. It is certainly true that for migration to be a possible solution individual governments would have to do a great deal more to integrate immigrants into society and help them to be present in all parts of the work force rather than just as unskilled workers._
